source_pr.title: br: use random port to start mock cluster.
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->
fix unstable ut in br pkg.

### What is changed and how it works?
1. remove multiple mock.Start()/mock.Stop() in restore test.
2. use net.Listen() to get a random port.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test


### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Mock cluster startup could fail due to port conflicts when allocating/listening on ports.
Restore client tests repeatedly starting/stopping the mock cluster risked leaving shared state/resources in an inconsistent state across test cases.
failure_signature: Mock cluster start flaked due to port conflicts / repeated start-stop lifecycle issues in tests.
root_cause_explanation: The instability came from resource conflicts and lifecycle management in tests: the mock cluster could start on a conflicting port, and tests repeatedly called `mock.Start()`/`mock.Stop()` in a way that could leave shared resources in a bad state. This PR stabilizes the suite by using `net.Listen("127.0.0.1:")` to pick available ephemeral ports and by centralizing the mock cluster start/stop into test setup/teardown.
fix_pattern: Use ephemeral ports (bind to `127.0.0.1:0`/`net.Listen`) and ensure proper setup/teardown to avoid port/resource conflicts between tests.

evidence: evidence: fix unstable ut in br pkg.
